formula_latex: \log_{10}(I_{arc}) = h_1 + h_2 V + h_3 \log_{10}(I_{bf}) + h_4 \log_{10}(G) + h_5 V \cdot I_{bf} + h_6 V \cdot G + h_7 V \cdot \log_{10}(I_{bf}) + h_8 V \cdot \log_{10}(G) + h_9 \log_{10}(I_{bf}) \cdot \log_{10}(G) + h_{10}
formula_plain_text: log10(I_arc) = h1 + h2·V + h3·log10(I_bf) + h4·log10(G) + h5·V·I_bf + h6·V·G + h7·V·log10(I_bf) + h8·V·log10(G) + h9·log10(I_bf)·log10(G) + h10. The 2018 arc-current model is a 10-term polynomial-in-V with cross-terms; coefficient ROW (h1..h10) is selected per (voltage_class, electrode_config) per Table 1. Note this functional form has explicit V coupling — distinguishes from the 2002 Annex F simplified regression where Iarc/Ibf was a simpler ratio. The 2018 SUPERSEDES 2002 Annex F for new studies.

symbols.meaning: Empirical coefficients per voltage class × electrode config (Table 1)

worked_examples.name: Annex D.1 (VCB at 480 V, Ibf=42kA)
worked_examples.source: IEEE 1584-2018 Annex D Example D.1 — approximate; verify when coefficients transcribed

implementation_notes: IEEE 1584-2018 §6 + Table 1 use V in kV and I_bf/I_arc in kA. Implementers MUST verify the unit convention against the licensed standard before computing — using V in volts will produce wildly wrong results given the V coupling in h2/h5/h6/h7/h8. The arcing-current variation (high/low bracket per §10.2) applies as a multiplicative envelope around this nominal Iarc. The 2018 arc-current values feed incident-energy-formula-2018.json (which expects I_arc in kA).
transcribed_by: DraftsMan skills repo — Sprint A.3 (Path Z2-ext)
license_note: Formula structure (10-term polynomial-in-V with cross-terms) is factual data per IEEE Std 1584-2018 Table 1 row schema. Coefficient values live in method-2018-tables-1-3-<class>V-coefficients.json (currently null pending source-vetted transcription).
